revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:地獄谷 (立山町)]] {{infobox Onsen}} {{coord|36|35|4|N|137|35|41|E|region:JP|display=title}} [[File:地獄谷火山ガス.JPG|thumb|Rising volcanic gas at Jigokudani]] '''Jigokudani''' (地獄谷) is a volcanic terrain located in {{ill|Tateyama, Toyama|en|Tateyama, Toyama|qid=Q670189|lt=Tateyama, Toyama|1=Tateyama, Toyama}}, {{ill|Nakaniikawa District, Toyama|en|Nakaniikawa District, Toyama|qid=Q1204336|lt=Nakaniikawa District, Toyama|1=Nakaniikawa District, Toyama}}, {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|Toyama Prefecture|qid=Q132929|lt=Toyama Prefecture|1=Toyama Prefecture}} (formerly {{ill|Etchū Province|ja|Etchū Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Etchū Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). It is situated at an altitude of '''2,300 meters'''.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://watchizu.gsi.go.jp/watchizu.html?longitude=137.5935361&latitude=36.58499722 |title=Geospatial Information Authority of Japan - Jigokudani |publisher=GSI Japan |accessdate=2011-09-05}} Jigokudani was formed as a result of '''Tateyama Volcano's''' final eruptions, along with {{ill|Mikurigaike Pond|ja|みくりが池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikurigaike Pond|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミクリガ池|1=Mikurigaike|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11341916}} and other geological formations. It was once a '''popular trekking spot''' on the {{ill|Murododaira|ja|室堂平|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murododaira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 室堂|1=Murodō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11452989}} hiking route. In the past, there were '''hot spring lodges''' near the '''fumarole zone''', but they were '''closed due to volcanic gas hazards'''. Since '''2011, the entire Jigokudani area has been off-limits''' due to '''increased volcanic activity'''. Several accommodations in the area still '''utilize hot spring water sourced from Jigokudani'''. == History == Although '''hot springs''' have long been known to exist in '''Jigokudani''', they were '''feared rather than utilized''' due to their association with '''{{ill|Tateyama Shugendō|ja|立山修験|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Shugendō|1=Tateyama Shugen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597829}}''', one of Japan’s most prominent '''mountain faiths'''. According to local beliefs, the site was considered '''a real-world manifestation of Hell'''. The '''"Eight Great Hells"''' (八大地獄) were named in the area, and it was believed that '''wrongdoers''' would fall into one of them as punishment. In '''1947''', a '''scientific analysis''' of the hot springs was conducted, confirming that the waters were suitable for '''bathing'''. That same year, the first '''hot spring lodge''', '''"Hōjiso"''', was built '''right in the middle of the geothermal field'''. By '''1954''', the '''{{ill|Tateyama Cable Car|en|Tateyama Cable Car|qid=Q11597852|lt=Tateyama Cable Car|1=Tateyama Cable Car}}''' was in operation, and by '''1955''', the '''Tateyama Highland Bus''' had extended to '''Midagahara''', later reaching '''Murododaira'''. This improved accessibility led to an '''influx of mountaineers, hikers, and skiers''', transitioning Tateyama from a '''sacred pilgrimage site''' to a more '''recreational destination'''. With the '''opening of new roads''', numerous '''mountain lodges''' were constructed, including '''Yūzansō (1955), Raichōsō (1955, old location), Lodge Tateyama Renpō (1957), and Mikurigaike Onsen (1957)'''. The '''Kanazawa University Summer Medical Clinic''' (旧) was also established in '''1956'''. The '''Alpine Route''' was completed in '''1971''', further increasing tourism. However, as '''volcanic gas risks''' became more evident, '''Hōjiso was relocated in 1975''', '''Yūzansō closed''', and the '''medical clinic moved''' to different locations. Eventually, '''all buildings in the geothermal zone were demolished''', leaving only '''remnants of former bathhouses'''. == Access == [[File:Murodo and Jigokudani from Mount Tate.jpg|thumb|250px|View of {{ill|Murodō|en|Murodō|qid=Q11452989|lt=Murododaira|1=Murodō}} and Jigokudani from {{ill|Mount Tate|en|Mount Tate|qid=Q2977103|lt=Mount Tate|1=Mount Tate}}
{{ill|Murodo Station|ja|室堂駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murodo Station|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 室堂ターミナル|1=Murodō Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6938821}} (center-left), {{ill|Mikurigaike Pond|ja|みくりが池|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikurigaike Pond|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミクリガ池|1=Mikurigaike|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11341916}} (center), and the '''Jigokudani geothermal area''' (upper-right)]] * '''Railway:''' '''Tateyama Kurobe Kankō''''s '''Tateyama Tunnel Trolleybus''' – '''15 to 50-minute walk from Murodo Station'''. * '''Bus:''' '''Tateyama Highland Bus''' (same location as the '''Trolleybus stop'''). Due to its extreme conditions, '''Jigokudani (Tateyama) is designated a "Transportation Difficulty Zone" by Japan Post''', meaning '''postal deliveries are not available'''.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.post.japanpost.jp/about/yakkan/1-7.pdf |title=Japan Post - Transportation Difficulty Zones |accessdate=2022-05-01 |publisher=[[Japan Post]] |date=2022-02-21}} == Onsen lodges == [[File:Jigokudani Onsen Toyama.JPG|thumb|right|240px|Jigokudani's accommodation facilities. From left: '''Raichōsō, Raichōzawa Hyutte, Lodge Tateyama Renpō'''. The barren area behind Raichōsō is '''Jigokudani'''.]] There are '''four onsen lodges''' around '''Murodo''', utilizing '''three geothermal sources''' from '''Jigokudani'''. Listed in order of proximity to '''Murodo Station''': {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:small;" ! '''Lodge Name''' !! '''Hot Spring Name''' !! '''Source Location'''All locations are within '''Tateyama Town, Nakaniikawa District, Toyama Prefecture'''. !! '''Spring Type''' !! '''Flow Rate''' !! '''Temperature''' !! '''pH''' !! '''Dissolved Minerals''' !! '''Notes''' |- | {{ill|Mikurigaike Onsen|ja|みくりが池温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikurigaike Onsen|1=Mikurigaike Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11278813}} || Jigokudani Onsen (1)
Mikurigaike Onsen || Jigokudani, Murododaira || Simple Acidic Spring || 60L/min || 43.5°C || 2.2 || 337mg/kg || |- | {{ill|Raichōsō|ja|雷鳥荘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raichōsō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} || Jigokudani Onsen (3)
Raichō Onsen || Bunasaka (National Forest) || Simple Spring || 49.7L/min || 44.2°C || 3.8 || 283mg/kg || |- | {{ill|Raichōzawa Hyutte|ja|雷鳥沢ヒュッテ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Raichōzawa Hyutte|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} || Jigokudani Onsen (4)
Raichōzawa Onsen || rowspan="2"|Bunasaka (National Forest, Jigokudani) || rowspan="2"|Simple Sulfur Spring || rowspan="2"|25L/min || rowspan="2"|51.5°C || rowspan="2"|5.54 || rowspan="2"|279.1mg/kg || rowspan="2"|Sister lodges with '''Lodge Tateyama Renpō''' (since 2006). | |- | {{ill|Lodge Tateyama Renpō|ja|ロッジ立山連峰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lodge Tateyama Renpō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- | | | | | | | | | |} == Gallery == File:MIKURIGAIKE-ONSEN.JPG|Mikurigaike Onsen, using Jigokudani’s geothermal water File:Raicho sou.JPG|Raichōsō, using Jigokudani’s geothermal water File:Raichoso Raichozawa Hutte and Lodge Tateyamarenpo.jpg|Raichōsō (top), Raichōzawa Hyutte (center), Lodge Tateyama Renpō (bottom), with steam rising from '''Jigokudani''' in the background File:Jigokudani and Mount Okudainichi 1995-08-19.jpg|Jigokudani and {{ill|Mount Okudainichi|ja|奥大日岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Okudainichi|qq=}} File:Jodosan from Morpdpnokkoshi 1995-8-20.jpg|Jigokudani and {{ill|Murodō|en|Murodō|qid=Q11452989|lt=Murododaira|1=Murodō}}, with '''Jōdosan''' in the background == See Also == * {{ill|Mount Tate|en|Mount Tate|qid=Q2977103|lt=Tateyama|1=Mount Tate}} * {{ill|Murodō|en|Murodō|qid=Q11452989|lt=Murododaira|1=Murodō}} * {{ill|Tateyama Jigokudani Hydrogen Sulfide Poisoning Incident|ja|立山地獄谷硫化水素中毒事故|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateyama Jigokudani Hydrogen Sulfide Poisoning Incident|1=Tateyama Jigokudani Hydrogen Sulfide Poisoning Incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q122290854}} == Notes == == References == == External links == * [http://www.mikuri.com/ Mikurigaike Onsen Official Site] * [http://www.raichoso.com/ Raichōsō Official Site] {{Onsen}} {{translated page|ja|地獄谷 (立山町)|version=101835707|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:地獄谷 (立山町)]] [[zh:高湯温泉]] [[zh:殺生石]] [[en:Sulfur springs]] [[ja:硫黄泉]] [[ja:地獄谷 (立山)]] {{Commons category|Jigokudani (Toyama)}} {{wikidata link|Q11424487}} [[Category:Geography of Tateyama Town]] [[Category:Hard-to-Access Areas]] [[Category:Onsens]] [[Category:Sulfur]] [[Category:Sulfur spring]] [[Category:Tateyama Kurobe Alpine Route]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
